message:
fix(apple-reminders): handle macOS carriage-return line separators in AppleScript output

Live Mac E2E testing revealed that macOS AppleScript output uses \r
(bare carriage returns) as line separators, not \n. The old code split
on \n and missed every record.

- Extract inline parsing closures into testable free functions:
  normalize_script_output() converts \r\n and \r to \n,
  parse_list_output() and parse_reminder_output() handle tab-delimited
  records
- run_invocation() now normalizes output before returning
- Refactor list_lists() and list_reminders() to use new free functions
- Add six new tests validating CR-delimited records matching exact shape
  of live macOS artifacts including Unicode narrow no-break space in dates
- Sort serde_json imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
